title: Sabazia annua
taxonomy: Sabazia annua is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to South Africa. It belongs to the genus Sabazia, which consists of annual plants. The plant is also known as annual Sabazia. Within the family Asteraceae, it is placed in the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Sabazia annua, also known as annual sabazia or Mexican sage, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico and parts of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve sleep, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Sabazia annua include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sabazia annua is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
